model_overview.summary: For broad questions about what the current model is, start with a tiny overview: identify the project root, check one or two domain signals, and sample a small material vocabulary.
whenToUse: what can you tell me about the model
give me an overview of the model
what kind of model is this
recommendedPatterns: MATCH (p:IfcProject) RETURN id(p) AS node_id, p.Name AS project_name, p.GlobalId AS global_id LIMIT 1
MATCH (:IfcRelAssociatesMaterial)--(material:IfcMaterial) RETURN DISTINCT material.Name AS material_name LIMIT 12
cautions: Keep the first pass tiny. Do not start an overview answer with a broad unlabeled scan or deep multi-join query.
Use one or two domain signals only, then explain what they imply instead of enumerating the whole model.
avoidPatterns: Broad unlabeled exploration before checking IfcProject.
Large product-family scans or graph walks as the first overview step.

project_seed.summary: Use the IFC project root as the most reliable semantic graph seed.
whenToUse: seed the graph from the project
recommendedPatterns: MATCH (p:IfcProject) RETURN id(p) AS node_id LIMIT 1
avoidPatterns: Broad unlabeled scans before checking IfcProject.

named_furniture_discovery.summary: Find furnishing candidates with a small label scan before adding text filters.
recommendedPatterns: MATCH (n:IfcFurniture) RETURN id(n) AS node_id, n.GlobalId AS global_id, n.Name AS name, n.ObjectType AS object_type LIMIT 25
MATCH (n:IfcSystemFurnitureElement) RETURN id(n) AS node_id, n.GlobalId AS global_id, n.Name AS name, n.ObjectType AS object_type LIMIT 25
cautions: Inspect names and object types first.
avoidPatterns: Large text-filter predicates as the first step.

relation_summary.summary: Use a plain local relation summary before filtered exploration.
whenToUse: what relations are slabs connected to
recommendedPatterns: MATCH (slab:IfcSlab)-[r]-(other) RETURN type(r) AS relation, count(*) AS connections ORDER BY connections DESC LIMIT 24
cautions: Narrow only after the first relation summary.
avoidPatterns: Complex WHERE filters on the first query.

roof_renderable_descendants.summary: Resolve roof slabs when the user wants visible roof elements.
recommendedPatterns: MATCH (:IfcRoof)<--(:IfcRelAggregates)-->(slab:IfcSlab) RETURN DISTINCT slab.GlobalId AS global_id LIMIT 200
MATCH (:IfcRoof)<--(:IfcRelAggregates)-->(slab:IfcSlab) RETURN DISTINCT id(slab) AS node_id LIMIT 24
avoidPatterns: Trying to hide an IfcRoof directly with element actions.

bounded_descendant_discovery.summary: Use a small, relation-constrained variable-length traversal to discover candidate descendants when the exact one-hop structure is unclear.
recommendedPatterns: MATCH (root)-[:RELATED_OBJECTS|RELATED_ELEMENTS*1..3]-(n) RETURN DISTINCT n.declared_entity AS entity, n.GlobalId AS global_id, n.Name AS name LIMIT 40
MATCH (root)-[:RELATING_OBJECT|RELATED_OBJECTS|RELATING_STRUCTURE|RELATED_ELEMENTS*1..3]-(n) RETURN DISTINCT n.declared_entity AS entity, n.GlobalId AS global_id, n.Name AS name LIMIT 40
cautions: Keep the range bounded and the relation family as narrow as you can.
Use the varlen walk to discover candidates, then follow up with a simpler query on the concrete products you found.
avoidPatterns: Bare `[*]` or `[*1..3]` walks when the relation family is already known.
Open-ended `*0..` traversal as a first exploration step.

material_scan.summary: List named materials first, then refine if the answer is too broad.
whenToUse: what is the house built of
recommendedPatterns: MATCH (:IfcRelAssociatesMaterial)--(material:IfcMaterial) RETURN DISTINCT material.Name AS material_name LIMIT 24
cautions: Be explicit when material evidence is indirect.
avoidPatterns: Speculative multi-join material tables as a first step.
